briefdescription.para: Given N/A

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_create() is called with a limited allocator with no memory
Then a null pointer is returned

briefdescription.para: Given N/A

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_create() is called with an allocator
Then a non-null pointer to the network (can_net_t) is returned, network default parameters are set

briefdescription.para: Test group for can_net_t tests.
Given a pointer to the network (can_net_t) with multiple receivers and a timer

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_destroy() is called
Then the network is destroyed

briefdescription.para: Given a null pointer to the network (can_net_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_destroy() is called
Then nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_get_time() is called with no memory area to store the return value
Then nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t) with no timers

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_time() is called with a time interval
Then 0 is returned, the requested time is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t) with a timer with a callback set

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_time() is called with a time interval less than the timer's trigger value
Then 0 is returned, the requested time is set, the timer callback is not called

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t) with a timer with a callback set

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_time() is called with a time interval greater than the timer's trigger value
Then 0 is returned, the requested time is set, the timer callback is called once

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t) with a timer with a callback returning an error set

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_time() is called with a time interval greater than the timer's trigger value
Then -1 is returned, the requested time is set, the timer callback is called once

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t) with a timer without a callback

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_time() is called with a time interval greater than the timer's trigger value
Then 0 is returned, the requested time is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t) with a timer with at least one second interval and a callback set

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_time() is called with a time interval greater than the timer's trigger value
Then 0 is returned, the requested time is set, the timer callback is called once

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t) with a timer with interval less than one second and a callback set

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_time() is called with a time interval greater than the timer's trigger value
Then 0 is returned, the requested time is set, the timer callback is called once

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t) with a timer with no start time value, a non-zero interval and a callback set

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_time() is called with a time interval few times greater than the timer's interval value
Then 0 is returned, the requested time is set, the timer callback is called multiple times

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t) with two timers with a callback returning error set

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_time() is called with a time interval greater than the timer's interval value
Then -1 is returned, the requested time is set, the timer callback is called twice

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t) with two timers with different start values and the next timer callback set

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_time() is called with a time interval between the timers start values
Then 0 is returned, the next timer callback is called once

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_get_next_func() is called with no memory area to store the result
Then nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (co_net_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_next_func() is called with pointers to the next timer callback and a user-specified data
Then requested pointers are set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (can_net_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_get_send_func() is called with no memory area to store the results
Then nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (can_net_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_send_func() is called with pointers to the send function and a user-specified data
Then the requested pointers are set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (can_net_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_get_active_bus() is called
Then the active bus ID is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (can_net_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_set_active_bus() is called with a bus ID
Then the active bus ID is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (can_net_t) with one receiver

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_recv() is called with a pointer to a CAN message and a bus ID of the active bus
Then 1 is returned, the receiver callback is called once

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (can_net_t) with no receivers

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_recv() is called with a pointer to a CAN message and a bus ID of the active bus
Then 1 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (can_net_t) with three receivers - two with a callback function returning an error and one with no callback

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_recv() is called with a pointer to a CAN message and a bus ID of the active bus
Then -1 is returned, the timer callback is called twice

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (can_net_t) with one receiver

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_recv() is called with a pointer to a CAN message and a bus ID of an inactive bus
Then 0 is returned, the receiver callback is not called

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the receiver (can_recv_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_recv_get_alloc() is called
Then allocator is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (can_net_t) with a callback send function set

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_send() is called with a CAN message
Then 0 is returned, the send function is called once

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the network (can_net_t) with a callback send function not set

detaileddescription.para: When can_net_send() is called with a CAN message
Then -1 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the timer (can_timer_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_timer_get_alloc() is called
Then allocator is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a null pointer to the timer (can_timer_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_timer_destroy() is called
Then nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the timer (can_timer_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_timer_get_func() is called with no memory area to store the result
Then nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the timer (can_timer_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_timer_set_func() is called with pointers to a timer callback function and a user-specified data
Then requested pointers are set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the timer (can_timer_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_timer_start() is called with a pointer to the network and no start nor interval for the timer
Then nothing is changed calls can_timer_stop()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the timer (can_timer_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_timer_timeout() is called with a pointer to the network and a timeout value
Then 0 is returned, the timer is started with a given timeout calls can_net_get_time() calls can_timer_start()

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the timer (can_timer_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_timer_timeout() is called with a pointer to the network and a negative timeout value
Then 0 is returned, the timer is not started calls can_timer_stop()

briefdescription.para: Given a null pointer to the receiver (can_recv_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_recv_destroy() is called
Then nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a null pointer to the receiver (can_recv_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_recv_get_func() is called with no memory area to store the results
Then nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to the receiver (can_recv_t)

detaileddescription.para: When can_recv_set_func() is called with pointers to receive function and the user-specified data
Then requested pointers are set

briefdescription.para: This file is part of the CANopen Library Unit Test Suite.

detaileddescription.para:
2020-2021 N7 Space Sp. z o.o.
Unit Test Suite was developed under a programme of, and funded by, the European Space Agency.
